mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly increasing present valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0%, labor market deterioration signals impending recessionary pressures and declining corporate earnings, causing negative forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 8.0 during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and capital demand expansion, which drives positive quarterly S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60d returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals supply-side contraction and weakening economic momentum, which reduces corporate profit expectations and leads to negative short-term S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5d returns in >60% of cases during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 210.0 force market expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL values above 210.0 are followed by positive 20d returns in >50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate indicates robust labor market tightening, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive quarterly S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS/UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with stronger corporate revenue fundamentals and improved profit margins, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no predictive power for 5d returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production above 95.0 indicates restored manufacturing capacity utilization and robust supply-side momentum, which supports corporate profit margins and drives positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in >55% of instances when INDPRO exceeds 95.0 during recovery phases.
